perturbation.original_text: 1. "Confidential Information" is that confidential, proprietary, and trade secret information being disclosed by the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "Protected Data" is that confidential, proprietary, and trade secret information being disclosed by the disclosing party pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Protected Data'. This creates an in-text contradiction because subsequent sections refer to 'Confidential Information', but the initial definition uses 'Protected Data', leading to ambiguity as to what the agreement covers and a mismatch on the terminology on the following sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Except as set forth in this Section 2, all Confidential Information shall be in tangible form and shall be marked as Confidential or proprietary information of the disclosing party. If the Confidential Information is disclosed orally or visually, it shall be identified as such at the time of disclosure and confirmed in a writing to the recipient within thirty (30) days of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in the definition. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because the contract now uses 'Confidential Information' in Section 2 without it being defined, while 'Protected Data' is defined, leading to ambiguity as to which information is covered. Therefore, there is a clear and direct conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Confidential Information of the other party only to those of its employees, consultants, advisors and investors who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Confidential Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Sensitive Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Sensitive Information of the other party only to those of its employees, consultants, advisors and investors who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Sensitive Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Here the term 'Confidential Information' is replaced with 'Sensitive Information'. Throughout the document, 'Confidential Information' is used, and this switch introduces confusion and inconsistency. This contradiction occurs because the defined term is 'Protected Data' (from the first perturbation), yet this section uses 'Sensitive Information' creating further ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties agrees that it will not make use of, disseminate, or in any way disclose any Sensitive Information of the other party to any person, firm or business, except to the extent necessary for negotiations, discussions, and consultations with personnel or authorized representatives of the other party and any purpose the other party may hereafter authorize in writing. Each of the parties agrees that it shall disclose Sensitive Information of the other party only to those of its employees, consultants, advisors and investors who need to know such information and who have previously agreed, either as a condition to employment or in order to obtain the Sensitive Information, to be bound by terms and conditions substantially similar to those of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Confidential Information', which is replaced by 'Sensitive Information' in the changed text. This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text because both use 'Sensitive Information' but 'Confidential Information' might be defined elsewhere. Because the changed text alters a key term used in another section, and introduces an inconsistent term (Sensitive Information), there is a clear contradiction. The perturbation type is Misaligned Terminology, which by definition introduces a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Confidential Information which is (a) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party, (d) independently developed by the receiving party without use of any Confidential Information and by persons who have not had access to any Confidential Information (e) generally made available to third parties without any restriction on disclosure, or (f) communicated in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as otherwise required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement (provided that the party so disclosing has provided the other party with a reasonable opportunity to seek protective legal treatment for such Confidential Information).
perturbation.changed_text: 4. There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Company Data which is (a) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party, (d) independently developed by the receiving party without use of any Company Data and by persons who have not had access to any Company Data (e) generally made available to third parties without any restriction on disclosure, or (f) communicated in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as otherwise required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement (provided that the party so disclosing has provided the other party with a reasonable opportunity to seek protective legal treatment for such Company Data).
perturbation.explanation: Replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Company Data'. The contract now uses 'Protected Data', 'Sensitive Information', and 'Company Data' to describe potentially the same thing without clarification. This undermines the purpose of the NDA and creates ambiguity for all of the other sections which depend on that definition, especially since subsection (d) mentions 'Company Data' and 'Confidential Information' in the same sentence
perturbation.contradicted_text: There shall be no liability for disclosure or use of Company Data which is (a) in the public domain through no fault of the receiving party (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on use or disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party, (d) independently developed by the receiving party without use of any Company Data and by persons who have not had access to any Company Data (e) generally made available to third parties without any restriction on disclosure, or (f) communicated in response to a valid order by a court or other governmental body, as otherwise required by law, or as necessary to establish the rights of either party under this Agreement (provided that the party so disclosing has provided the other party with a reasonable opportunity to seek protective legal treatment for such Company Data).
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information', which is then replaced with 'Company Data' in the changed text. The explanation states that this creates ambiguity because the contract now uses 'Protected Data', 'Sensitive Information', and 'Company Data' to describe potentially the same thing without clarification, undermining the purpose of the NDA. While the Changed Text and the Contradicted text might be in the same location, based on the explanation, the change of the text introduces a contradiction based on Misaligned Terminology definition. Therefore the change causes a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Confidential Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Confidential Information, and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Proprietary Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Proprietary Information, and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' in this section adds to the confusion. Now, the contract uses 'Protected Data', 'Sensitive Information', 'Company Data', and 'Proprietary Information', making it unclear which data is protected and to what extent. This directly contradicts the purpose of an NDA which is to clearly define what information is confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties agrees that it shall treat all Proprietary Information of the other party with the same degree of care as it accords to its own Proprietary Information, and each of the parties represents that it exercises reasonable care to protect its own Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' introduces misaligned terminology as described in the Perturbation Explanation. The contract now uses multiple terms ('Protected Data', 'Sensitive Information', 'Company Data', and 'Proprietary Information') to refer to protected data, creating ambiguity and making it unclear which data is protected under the NDA. This contradicts the purpose of the NDA, which is to clearly define what information is confidential. The contradicted text is the same, but based on the Perturbation Explanation, it adds to the contradiction because the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text both mention 'Proprietary Information' without it being clearly defined what it means relative to other similar terms in the document. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed, provided, however, that each party's obligations under Sections 2 and 3 with respect to Confidential Information of the other party which it has previously received shall continue unless and until such Confidential Information falls within Section 4. Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party. Neither party acquires any licenses under any intellectual property rights of the other party under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement shall govern all communications between the parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed, provided, however, that each party's obligations under Sections 2 and 3 with respect to Protected Data of the other party which it has previously received shall continue unless and until such Protected Data falls within Section 4. Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party. Neither party acquires any licenses under any intellectual property rights of the other party under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Finally, changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' in section 8, while also referencing the earlier changes in sections 2 and 3, highlights the overall confusion. 'Protected Data' was the original replacement in section 1, but the document now uses various terms interchangeably, invalidating the legal effect of the NDA due to lack of clarity. This section is contradicted by the fact that sections 2 and 3 are referencing to 'Sensitive Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall govern all communications between the parties that are made during the period from the effective date of this Agreement to the date on which either party receives from the other written notice that subsequent communications shall not be so governed, provided, however, that each party's obligations under Sections 2 and 3 with respect to Protected Data of the other party which it has previously received shall continue unless and until such Protected Data falls within Section 4. Neither party shall communicate any information to the other in violation of the proprietary rights of any third party. Neither party acquires any licenses under any intellectual property rights of the other party under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in section 8. The explanation states this change, along with prior changes in sections 2 and 3, creates confusion by using terms interchangeably ('Protected Data' in section 1, 'Sensitive Information' in sections 2 and 3, and now 'Protected Data' again in section 8). This contradicts the requirement for clarity in the NDA, leading to a potential invalidation of the agreement. The contradicted text includes a reference to 'Protected Data' in the same section, but it relies on consistent definitions throughout the document, which the changed text undermines, fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.
